item.text: I’ll inspect the fixture app’s manifests and lockfiles first, then map vulnerable packages to fixed versions before changing anything. After that I’ll update only the dependency data needed, run the test suite, and document each package in `FIXES.md`.

# `make test` rebuilds every fixture from clean installs and runs its test

PYTHON ?= python3.12

.PHONY: test test-bigapp clean

test: test-bigapp ## Build + test the fixture from clean

test-bigapp: ## Large Maven fixture (vendored Grouper 4.x, 13-module reactor): compile + build, no test run
	# The reactor's tests need external infrastructure, so this compiles and
	# builds the modules without running them. -DskipTests (NOT
	# -Dmaven.test.skip): the `grouper` module depends on grouperClient's
	# test-jar, which -Dmaven.test.skip would not build.
	cd fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent && mvn -B -DskipTests install

clean: ## Remove build artifacts and virtualenvs
	find fixtures/bigapp -type d -name target -prune -exec rm -rf {} +

?? AGENTS.md
?? CLAUDE.md
?? GEMINI.md

item.text: The fixture is a Maven reactor under `fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent`; `make test` runs `mvn -B -DskipTests install` there. I’m going to inventory the effective dependency versions from the parent and use an external vulnerability source rather than relying on Maven’s normal build tooling, since Maven itself won’t audit this project.

item.text: The dependency versions are centralized in the parent POM, but the real vulnerable set includes transitives. I’m generating a resolved dependency tree for the reactor and will query OSV against the resolved Maven coordinates so we don’t miss packages that are not directly declared.

item.text: I have a full reactor tree now. Next I’m querying OSV in batches using the resolved `groupId:artifactId` and version pairs; this should surface both direct and transitive Maven advisories.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import re,json,urllib.request
coords=set()
for line in open('/tmp/bigapp-dependency-tree.txt'):
    s=line.strip()
    if not s or s.startswith('['): continue
    s=re.sub(r'"'^[+'"\\\\|\\- ]+','',s)
    parts=s.split(':')
    if len(parts)<4: continue
    group,artifact=parts[0],parts[1]
    if group.startswith('edu.internet2.middleware.grouper'): continue
    # strip tree symbols on group if any remain
    group=group.lstrip('+-\\\\ ')
    version=parts[-2] if len(parts)>=5 else parts[-1]
    if version.endswith('SNAPSHOT'): continue
    coords.add((group,artifact,version))
queries=[{\"package\":{\"ecosystem\":\"Maven\",\"name\":f\"{g}:{a}\"},\"version\":v} for g,a,v in sorted(coords)]
print('coords',len(coords))
allv=[]
for i in range(0,len(queries),100):
    data=json.dumps({\"queries\":queries[i:i+100]}).encode()
    req=urllib.request.Request('https://api.osv.dev/v1/querybatch',data=data,headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'})
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req,timeout=60) as resp:
        out=json.load(resp)
    for q,res in zip(queries[i:i+100],out.get('results',[])):
        vulns=res.get('vulns') or []
        if vulns:
            allv.append((q['package']['name'],q['version'],[(v['id'],v.get('summary','')) for v in vulns]))
print(json.dumps(allv,indent=2))
PY"

/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import re,json,urllib.request
coords=set()
for line in open('/tmp/bigapp-dependency-tree.txt'):
    s=line.strip()
    if not s or s.startswith('['): continue
    s=re.sub(r'"'^[+'"\\\\|\\- ]+','',s)
    parts=s.split(':')
    if len(parts)<4: continue
    group,artifact=parts[0],parts[1]
    if group.startswith('edu.internet2.middleware.grouper'): continue
    # strip tree symbols on group if any remain
    group=group.lstrip('+-\\\\ ')
    version=parts[-2] if len(parts)>=5 else parts[-1]
    if version.endswith('SNAPSHOT'): continue
    coords.add((group,artifact,version))
queries=[{\"package\":{\"ecosystem\":\"Maven\",\"name\":f\"{g}:{a}\"},\"version\":v} for g,a,v in sorted(coords)]
print('coords',len(coords))
allv=[]
for i in range(0,len(queries),100):
    data=json.dumps({\"queries\":queries[i:i+100]}).encode()
    req=urllib.request.Request('https://api.osv.dev/v1/querybatch',data=data,headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'})
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req,timeout=60) as resp:
        out=json.load(resp)
    for q,res in zip(queries[i:i+100],out.get('results',[])):
        vulns=res.get('vulns') or []
        if vulns:
            allv.append((q['package']['name'],q['version'],[(v['id'],v.get('summary','')) for v in vulns]))
print(json.dumps(allv,indent=2))
PY"

item.text: OSV flagged 24 package/version pairs. I’m pulling the affected ranges and dependency paths now so the changes can be made at the nearest managed dependency instead of scattering overrides blindly.

item.text: Some advisories have straightforward fixed versions, but two important cases need care: `commons-httpclient` reports no fixed release, and OSV reports the Hibernate advisory with no fixed release. I’m checking whether the Jackson 3 fixed coordinate exists under this project’s current group before deciding whether that one can be solved by a simple version bump.

<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
    xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/maven-v4_0_0.xsd">

    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <parent>
        <groupId>edu.internet2.middleware.grouper</groupId>
        <artifactId>grouper-ws-parent</artifactId>
        <version>4.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
        <relativePath>../../grouper-ws</relativePath>
    </parent>

    <name>Grouper WS</name>
    <description>Internet2 Groups Management WS Core</description>
    <artifactId>grouper-ws</artifactId>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>
    <version>4.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>

    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>${project.groupId}</groupId>
            <artifactId>grouper</artifactId>
            <version>${project.version}</version>
        </dependency>
<!--
        <dependency>
            <groupId>javax.servlet</groupId>
            <artifactId>servlet-api</artifactId>
            <version>2.4</version>
            <scope>provided</scope>
        </dependency>
-->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>javax.servlet</groupId>
            <artifactId>javax.servlet-api</artifactId>
            <scope>provided</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>commons-fileupload</groupId>
            <artifactId>commons-fileupload</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.axis2</groupId>
            <artifactId>axis2-kernel</artifactId>
            <version>1.6.4</version>
            <exclusions>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.apache.geronimo.specs</groupId>
                    <artifactId>geronimo-activation_1.1_spec</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.apache.geronimo.specs</groupId>
                    <artifactId>geronimo-javamail_1.4_spec</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.apache.geronimo.specs</groupId>
                    <artifactId>geronimo-jta_1.1_spec</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.apache.geronimo.specs</groupId>
                    <artifactId>geronimo-stax-api_1.0_spec</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.apache.geronimo.specs</groupId>
                    <artifactId>geronimo-ws-metadata_2.0_spec</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>javax.servlet</groupId>
                    <artifactId>servlet-api</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <!-- jsr311-api package javax.ws.rs.core conflicts with Jersey's which uses classes from jakarta.ws.rs-api-2.1.6 -->
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>javax.ws.rs</groupId>
                    <artifactId>jsr311-api</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>jaxen</groupId>
                    <artifactId>jaxen</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>jaxen</groupId>
            <artifactId>jaxen</artifactId>
            <version>2.0.0</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.axis2</groupId>
            <artifactId>axis2-transport-http</artifactId>
            <version>1.6.4</version>
            <exclusions>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.apache.httpcomponents</groupId>
                    <artifactId>httpcore</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>
        <!-- axis2-transport-http pulls in org.apache.httpcomponents:httpclient 4.5.3 which pulls httpcore 4.4.6,
             while aws-java-sdk-core pulls httpclient 4.5.5 -> httpcore 4.4.9 -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.httpcomponents</groupId>
            <artifactId>httpclient</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.axis2</groupId>
            <artifactId>axis2-transport-local</artifactId>
            <version>1.6.1</version>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
        </dependency>
        <!-- dependency>
            <groupId>io.swagger.core.v3</groupId>
            <artifactId>swagger-annotations</artifactId>
            <version>2.1.11</version>
        </dependency -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.swagger</groupId>
            <artifactId>swagger-annotations</artifactId>
            <version>1.6.3</version>
        </dependency>
        <!-- DEBUG SWAGGER JSON BUILD -->
        <!--   dependency>
            <groupId>com.github.kongchen</groupId>
            <artifactId>swagger-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            <version>3.1.8</version>
        </dependency -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.axis2</groupId>
            <artifactId>axis2-adb</artifactId>
            <version>1.6.1</version>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
            <exclusions>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.apache.geronimo.specs</groupId>
                    <artifactId>geronimo-activation_1.1_spec</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>commons-net</groupId>
            <artifactId>commons-net</artifactId>
            <version>3.9.0</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>junit</groupId>
            <artifactId>junit</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>${project.groupId}</groupId>
            <artifactId>grouper</artifactId>
            <version>${project.version}</version>
            <type>test-jar</type>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

        <!-- still undetermined dependencies (provided in lib dir but jdeps doesn't show any use)
        axis2-xmlbeans-1.6.1.jar
        axis2-adb-codegen-1.6.1.jar
        axis2-codegen-1.6.1.jar
        axis2-java2wsdl-1.6.1.jar
        axis2-xmlbeans-1.6.1.jar
        cron-parser-core-3.4.jar
        cron-parser-core-3.4.jar
        ddlutils-1.0.jar
        ehcache-core-2.4.8.jar
        stax-1.2.0.jar (stax-api is pulled in by ddlutils, suitable replacement?)
        xmlbeans-2.3.0.jar
        xmltooling-1.2.0.jar
        xom-1.1.jar
        -->

        <!-- Dependencies for SCIM 2 -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.unboundid.product.scim2</groupId>
            <artifactId>scim2-sdk-server</artifactId>
            <version>2.3.7</version>
            <exclusions>
                <!-- this has a dep on jackson-core 2.10.2; but jackson-datatype-jsr310 plus other grouper modules are
                already pulling in jackson-core 2.14.0 -->
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
                    <artifactId>jackson-core</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <!-- pulling in version 2.10.2; we want 2.14.0 to match jackson-core 2.14.0 in use by grouper -->
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.jaxrs</groupId>
                    <artifactId>jackson-jaxrs-base</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <!-- pulling in version 2.10.2; we want 2.14.0 to match jackson-core 2.14.0 in use by grouper -->
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.jaxrs</groupId>
                    <artifactId>jackson-jaxrs-json-provider</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>
        <!-- add in the newer version of 2 dependencies needed for scim2-sdk-server -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.jaxrs</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-jaxrs-base</artifactId>
            <version>2.18.2</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.jaxrs</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-jaxrs-json-provider</artifactId>
            <version>2.18.2</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.glassfish.jersey.containers</groupId>
            <artifactId>jersey-container-servlet</artifactId>
            <version>2.36</version>
        </dependency>
        <!-- without this, gets error "InjectionManagerFactory not found" -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.glassfish.jersey.inject</groupId>
            <artifactId>jersey-hk2</artifactId>
            <version>2.36</version>
        </dependency>
        <!-- without this, gets error "MessageBodyWriter not found for media type=application/scim+json" -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.glassfish.jersey.media</groupId>
            <artifactId>jersey-media-json-jackson</artifactId>
            <version>2.36</version>
            <exclusions>
                <!-- this has a dep on jackson-core 2.10.2; but jackson-datatype-jsr310 plus other grouper modules are
                already pulling in jackson-core 2.14.0 -->
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
                    <artifactId>jackson-annotations</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>

        </dependency>
        <!-- needed for LocalDateTime unmarshalling -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-datatype-jsr310</artifactId>
            <version>2.18.2</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>javax</groupId>
            <artifactId>javaee-api</artifactId>
            <version>7.0</version>
            <scope>provided</scope>
        </dependency>

        <!-- test dependencies -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.hamcrest</groupId>
            <artifactId>hamcrest-core</artifactId>
            <version>1.3</version>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.hamcrest</groupId>
            <artifactId>hamcrest-library</artifactId>
            <version>1.3</version>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.mockito</groupId>
            <artifactId>mockito-all</artifactId>
            <version>1.10.19</version>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.powermock</groupId>
            <artifactId>powermock-module-junit4</artifactId>
            <version>1.6.5</version>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.powermock</groupId>
            <artifactId>powermock-api-mockito</artifactId>
            <version>1.6.5</version>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

    </dependencies>
    <build>
        <sourceDirectory>src/grouper-ws</sourceDirectory>
        <testSourceDirectory>src/test</testSourceDirectory>
        
        <resources>
            <resource>
                <directory>conf</directory>
                <includes>
                    <!--include>grouper-ws.base.properties</include-->
                    <include>jaas.conf</include>
                    <include>krb5.example.conf</include>
                </includes>
            </resource>
        </resources>
        <testResources>
            <testResource>
                <directory>confForTest</directory>
            </testResource>
            <!--
            <testResource>
                <directory>../../grouper/confForTest</directory>
            </testResource>
            <testResource>
                <directory>../../grouper/conf</directory>
            </testResource>
            -->
        </testResources>

        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                <artifactId>build-helper-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <phase>generate-sources</phase>
                        <goals><goal>add-source</goal></goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <sources>
                                <source>src/grouper-ws_v1_6</source>
                                <source>src/grouper-ws_v2_0</source>
                                <source>src/grouper-ws_v2_1</source>
                                <source>src/grouper-ws_v2_2</source>
                                <source>src/grouper-ws_v2_3</source>
                                <source>src/grouper-ws_v2_4</source>
                                <source>src/grouper-ws_v2_5</source>
                            </sources>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-jar-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <archive>
                        <index>true</index>
                        <manifestEntries>
                            <Specification-Title>${project.name}</Specification-Title>
                            <Implementation-Vendor>${project.organization.name}</Implementation-Vendor>
                            <Implementation-Title>${project.artifactId}</Implementation-Title>
                            <Implementation-Version>${project.version}</Implementation-Version>
                            <Build-Timestamp>${maven.build.timestamp}</Build-Timestamp>
                        </manifestEntries>
                    </archive>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>com.github.kongchen</groupId>
                <artifactId>swagger-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>3.1.8</version>
                <configuration>
                    <!-- https://spaces.at.internet2.edu/x/xQEREg -->
                    <!-- temporarily set this to false and run this, then compare, merge -->
                    <!-- mvn -f grouper-ws/grouper-ws clean compile swagger:generate -->
                    <skipSwaggerGeneration>true</skipSwaggerGeneration>
                    <apiSources>
                      <apiSource>
                            <springmvc>false</springmvc>
                            <locations>
                              <location>edu.internet2.middleware.grouper.ws.rest.GrouperServiceRest</location>
                              <location>edu.internet2.middleware.grouper.ws.coresoap.GrouperService</location>
                            </locations>
                            <schemes><scheme>https</scheme></schemes>
                            <host>grouper.institution.edu</host>

item.text: The vulnerable web-service transitives come from old Axis2 artifacts, while the Netty set comes from `qpid-jms-client` and the Google issues come from the Google API client stack. I’m going to centralize explicit fixed-version overrides in the parent where possible, and only touch module POMs where a hardcoded direct dependency is the source.

item.text: `commons-httpclient` is a special case: it is end-of-line and the advisory has no fixed 3.x version, but this repo has a direct installer dependency on it. I’m checking whether the installer code actually imports that API; if it does, I’ll leave it documented as no-fixed-version rather than pretending a replacement is just a version bump.

item.text: I’m editing the POMs now. The main changes are fixed-version overrides for transitives, newer direct versions for Axis2/Jackson/commons-compress/jose4j, and replacing the Box SDK’s old `jdk15on` Bouncy Castle transitives with the already-managed `jdk18on` line.
The parent now manages the fixed transitive versions. I’m applying the module-level direct dependency edits next, then I’ll regenerate the tree to catch any leftovers or version conflicts.
The POM edits are in place. I’m resolving the reactor again now; this is where Maven will show whether any override introduced a binary or metadata conflict before we get to the full build.

item.text: The first resolve found that the old AWS SDK line pulls `software.amazon.ion:ion-java`, whose fixed version is published under a different group. I switched the AWS SDK 1.x modules to a newer 1.12.x release that no longer brings that vulnerable Ion artifact, then I’m resolving again.

item.text: The resolved dependency tree now builds successfully. I’m running the same OSV query against the updated tree to verify which advisories are gone and to identify only the packages that truly have no drop-in fixed coordinate.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import re,json,urllib.request
coords=set()
for line in open('/tmp/bigapp-dependency-tree-fixed.txt'):
    s=line.strip()
    if not s or s.startswith('['): continue
    s=re.sub(r'"'^[+'"\\\\|\\- ]+','',s)
    parts=s.split(':')
    if len(parts)<4: continue
    group,artifact=parts[0],parts[1]
    if group.startswith('edu.internet2.middleware.grouper'): continue
    version=parts[-2] if len(parts)>=5 else parts[-1]
    if version.endswith('SNAPSHOT'): continue
    coords.add((group,artifact,version))
queries=[{\"package\":{\"ecosystem\":\"Maven\",\"name\":f\"{g}:{a}\"},\"version\":v} for g,a,v in sorted(coords)]
allv=[]
for i in range(0,len(queries),100):
    req=urllib.request.Request('https://api.osv.dev/v1/querybatch',data=json.dumps({\"queries\":queries[i:i+100]}).encode(),headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'})
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req,timeout=60) as resp: out=json.load(resp)
    for q,res in zip(queries[i:i+100],out.get('results',[])):
        vulns=res.get('vulns') or []
        if vulns: allv.append((q['package']['name'],q['version'],[(v['id'],v.get('summary','')) for v in vulns]))
print('coords',len(coords))
print(json.dumps(allv,indent=2))
PY"

    req=urllib.request.Request('https://api.osv.dev/v1/querybatch',data=json.dumps({\"queries\":queries[i:i+100]}).encode(),headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'})

    for q,res in zip(queries[i:i+100],out.get('results',[])):
        vulns=res.get('vulns') or []
        if vulns: allv.append((q['package']['name'],q['version'],[(v['id'],v.get('summary','')) for v in vulns]))
print('coords',len(coords))
print(json.dumps(allv,indent=2))
PY"

item.text: The second scan shows most findings cleared. Two Netty classifier artifacts were still pinned because Maven dependency management needs classifier-specific entries, and Axis2’s newer stack introduced an older `woodstox-core`; I’m adding explicit overrides for those. The remaining findings after that are expected no-fixed-version or no-same-coordinate cases.
The classifier and Woodstox overrides are in. I’m regenerating and rescanning one more time before running the full `make test`.

item.text: The final OSV scan is down to five unresolved package/version pairs. Each is either no fixed release in OSV (`commons-httpclient`, `hibernate-core`) or no fixed version for the same Maven coordinate/API line (`jackson-databind` 2.x, `commons-lang` 2.x, `net.sf.json-lib` 2.x), so I’ll document those explicitly instead of claiming a false fix.

item.text: I removed an unused property left from the Ion investigation. Now I’m writing `FIXES.md` with one entry per vulnerable package from the original scan, including the packages that could not be fixed by a version-only change.
`FIXES.md` now records the full original finding set and the action taken for each package. I’m running the requested root `make test` now; this is the authoritative build check for the fixture.

`make test` is still running. It has compiled through the UI and web-service modules; Checkstyle is noisy in existing code but configured not to fail on those violations. I’m waiting for the remaining reactor modules to finish.

item.text:
Implemented the dependency remediation under `fixtures/` and documented it in [FIXES.md](/tmp/bomly-study-_ik0ralc/codex-bare-bigapp-4-1783757663/repo/FIXES.md:1).

Changed:
- Centralized fixed versions and transitive overrides in [grouper-parent/pom.xml](/tmp/bomly-study-_ik0ralc/codex-bare-bigapp-4-1783757663/repo/fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent/pom.xml:176), including Jackson, AWS SDK, protobuf, Nimbus, Guava, Netty, Woodstox, Axis2 transitives, MIME4J, Neethi, commons-compress, and jose4j.
- Updated hardcoded WS dependency versions to use the fixed parent-managed versions in [grouper-ws/pom.xml](/tmp/bomly-study-_ik0ralc/codex-bare-bigapp-4-1783757663/repo/fixtures/bigapp/grouper-ws/grouper-ws/pom.xml:60).
- Updated installer commons-compress in [grouper-installer/pom.xml](/tmp/bomly-study-_ik0ralc/codex-bare-bigapp-4-1783757663/repo/fixtures/bigapp/grouper-misc/grouper-installer/pom.xml:43).
- Updated Box jose4j and replaced old Bouncy Castle `jdk15on` transitives with `jdk18on` dependencies in [grouper-box/pom.xml](/tmp/bomly-study-_ik0ralc/codex-bare-bigapp-4-1783757663/repo/fixtures/bigapp/grouper-misc/grouper-box/pom.xml:45).

Verification:
- Regenerated and rescanned the resolved Maven tree with OSV.
- `make test` passed successfully at the repo root.

Remaining OSV findings are explicitly documented as not fixable by a version-only change: `commons-httpclient`, `commons-lang` 2.x, `net.sf.json-lib` 2.x, `hibernate-core`, and one Jackson databind advisory whose fixed release is on the Jackson 3 `tools.jackson.core` coordinate rather than the current `com.fasterxml` coordinate.
